# Phase 5.2: Diff-Layer Hand-Fill Affordance - Context

**Gathered:** 2026-06-25
**Status:** Ready for planning

<domain>
## Phase Boundary

Add a "soft-pair" pre-pass to `mlpstorage_py/system_description/diff.py::diff_node_dict_lists` so that
hand-filled fingerprint *scalar* values survive a re-run when the auto-collector still
returns `""` at the same position, while real hardware drift (collector returns a NEW
non-empty value that disagrees with the hand-fill) continues to raise `SystemDriftError`.

**As a** submitter who hand-fills a value into the auto-generated `systemname.yaml`
for a field the collector returned `""` for on the previous run (canonical example:
`chassis.model_name` on a generic Linux host without parseable DMI strings),
**I want to** be able to re-run the benchmark against the same results-dir
WITHOUT triggering `SystemDriftError E404`,
**so that** I can enrich the auto-detected client identity with human-known
context (model nicknames, hand-resolved chassis models, friendly hostnames)
while still being protected against real hardware drift.

**In scope:** the 7 scalar dotted-key fingerprint positions
(`chassis.cpu_model`, `chassis.cpu_qty`, `chassis.cpu_cores`,
`chassis.memory_capacity`, `chassis.model_name`,
`operating_system.name`, `operating_system.version`).

**Out of scope:** the 4 callable signature positions (`networking_sig`,
`sysctl_sig`, `environment_sig`, `drives_sig`) — they stay strict-match per
D-61. The user does not hand-edit these. The env/sysctl denylist audit is a
separate deferred concern tracked in
`.planning/todos/pending/phase-future-env-sysctl-fingerprint-audit.md`
(post-v1.0).

**Not changing:** the on-disk format, the writer (`write_systemname_yaml`),
LIFE-04 file-no-touch semantics, the D-38 fingerprint composition,
`_compute_fingerprint`'s contract (still returns the 11-tuple identity
function for general-purpose callers like dedup/sort/render — only
`diff_node_dict_lists` adds the soft-pair pre-pass).

</domain>

<decisions>
## Implementation Decisions

### Phase 5.2 decisions (D-60..D-66)

#### Reverse-direction case (recomputed non-empty + on-disk empty)
- **D-60 — INFO log per field, no DiffEntry, no SystemDriftError.** When the
  collector finally learns a value at a fingerprint position that the user
  did NOT hand-fill (on-disk was `""`), the diff layer emits a one-time
  INFO log on the operator's terminal of the shape
  `collector resolved chassis.model_name="Latitude 7420" (was "" on disk;
  on-disk file unchanged per LIFE-04 — manually update the YAML if you want
  to lock this value)`. NO `DiffEntry` is appended (so `DiffResult.empty`
  stays `True` and the lifecycle does NOT raise `SystemDriftError`). The
  on-disk file is not touched per LIFE-04. User sees the new knowledge once
  and can choose to hand-edit if they want to pin it. INFO level (not
  WARNING) — unobtrusive but visible in default CI/dev logs.

#### Signature-position handling
- **D-61 — Signatures are strict-match.** The 4 callable signature positions
  (`networking_sig`, `sysctl_sig`, `environment_sig`, `drives_sig`) are
  tuples computed from list-derived collector data. Soft-pair semantics
  apply ONLY to the 7 scalar dotted-key positions. An empty signature `()`
  on one side does NOT count as soft-pair-able; it must match the other
  side's signature exactly. Rationale: (a) users do not hand-edit
  `sysctl_sig` or its peers — there is no hand-fill use-case to support;
  (b) treating empty signatures as wildcards would risk conflating
  genuinely distinct machines whose hardware identity differs along the
  signature axis but happens to be `""` on a few scalar positions.

#### Pairing algorithm shape
- **D-62 — Two-pass pairing: exact-match first, then soft-pair the orphans.**
  Algorithm:
  1. Index both sides by `_compute_fingerprint(stanza)` (existing
     `on_disk_by_fp` / `in_memory_by_fp` dicts at `diff.py:235-236`).
  2. Pair fingerprints present on BOTH sides — existing behavior unchanged;
     these clients fall through to leaf-level comparison.
  3. Collect the remaining unmatched on-disk + in-memory orphans.
  4. SOFT-PAIR PASS (new): for each in-memory orphan whose fingerprint has
     at least one `""` position in the 7 scalar positions AND whose 4
     signature positions match exactly some on-disk orphan's signatures,
     find the unique on-disk orphan whose NON-EMPTY scalar fingerprint
     positions all align (positions where in-memory has `""` are ignored
     in the comparison). If exactly ONE such on-disk orphan exists AND
     it has not been soft-paired yet, treat them as the same client —
     fall through to leaf-level comparison (existing Pitfall 3(a) SER-02
     at `diff.py:272-280` handles the empty-vs-filled rule).
  5. Surviving orphans after the soft-pair pass emit `DiffEntry` per
     D-46/D-47 (unchanged behavior).
  Rationale: clean conceptual separation; each pass independently testable;
  preserves the existing exact-match fast path for the common steady-state
  case (no `""` positions); only orphans pay the O(N×M) soft-pair cost,
  and N, M ≤ orphan_count which is bounded by fleet size.

#### Soft-pair uniqueness (mirrors ROADMAP SC#5)
- **D-63 — Ambiguous soft-pair candidates fall back to orphan emission;
  never silently conflate.** If two or more on-disk orphans qualify as
  soft-pair candidates for the same in-memory orphan (their non-empty
  scalar positions all align with the in-memory orphan's non-empty
  positions, AND their signatures all match the in-memory orphan's
  signatures), the in-memory orphan emits a `<absent>` / `<present>`
  DiffEntry per D-46 and ALL candidate on-disk orphans emit
  `<present>` / `<absent>` DiffEntries per D-47. This is intentionally
  conservative: false soft-pairing across genuinely distinct machines
  would silently mask hardware drift, which is worse than a slightly
  noisier drift report. The user is given the chance to either hand-fill
  the scalar positions to disambiguate, or to investigate the genuine
  topology change.

#### Requirements landing
- **D-64 — HANDFILL-01 lands in REQUIREMENTS.md in the same commit as
  this CONTEXT.md.** Mirrors the D-50..D-59 pattern from Phase 5.1: the
  discuss-phase commit (a) writes 05.2-CONTEXT.md + 05.2-DISCUSSION-LOG.md,
  (b) appends `### Hand-Fill Affordance (HANDFILL)` to REQUIREMENTS.md
  with HANDFILL-01, (c) updates the Traceability table to map
  HANDFILL-01 → Phase 5.2, (d) ROADMAP.md Phase 5.2 entry already shows
  `**Requirements:** HANDFILL-01` (set during phase insertion at commit
  `08e00ba`). Closes the requirements loop before plan-phase runs — the
  planner's Requirements Coverage Gate then has a real ID to enforce.

#### Plan structure
- **D-65 — Single plan, RED → GREEN cadence.** Total scope is ~150-200
  lines including tests. Single plan `05.2-01-PLAN.md` with two tasks:
  (a) RED — failing tests in `tests/unit/test_diff.py` for hand-fill
  survival, real-drift-still-raises, ambiguous fallback, reverse-direction
  INFO log, signature-position strict-match, two-pass exact-match-first
  preservation. (b) GREEN — production-code edit in `diff.py` adding the
  soft-pair pre-pass per D-62, the reverse-direction INFO log per D-60,
  and the strict-signature filter per D-61. No REFACTOR step needed at
  this size; if the helper extraction is clean, it goes into the GREEN
  commit. RED-first discipline per `feedback_tdd_red_first_even_for_shipped_fixes`
  (this is a NEW feature, not a re-locking of a shipped fix — but the
  RED-first habit still applies).

#### Helper function shape
- **D-66 — Helper function lives in `diff.py` as a module-level private.**
  Recommended shape (planner has discretion on exact signature):
  ```python
  def _soft_pair_orphans(
      in_memory_orphans: list[dict],
      on_disk_orphans: list[dict],
  ) -> tuple[list[tuple[dict, dict]], list[dict], list[dict]]:
      """Return (paired, remaining_in_memory_orphans, remaining_on_disk_orphans).

      Per D-62 soft-pair rules. Signatures must match exactly per D-61;
      scalar positions with "" on the in-memory side are ignored in the
      comparison. Uniqueness enforced per D-63 — ambiguous in-memory
      orphans stay unpaired and propagate to surviving orphans for
      D-46/D-47 emission.
      """
  ```
  Naming may differ — planner is free to pick something that reads
  cleanly alongside the existing `_compute_fingerprint`,
  `_render_fingerprint`, `_flatten_to_paths` private helpers in `diff.py`.

### Claude's Discretion

- Exact wording of the D-60 INFO log message — `cluster_collector` log message
  style is the closest analog; pick something that matches existing operator-
  facing log conventions in the project. Include the field path
  (`chassis.model_name`), the resolved value, the previous on-disk value
  (`""`), and a hint that the on-disk file is intentionally unchanged.
- Naming of new test methods in `tests/unit/test_diff.py` — reuse the existing
  `TestDiff*` class structure (see line 128 for current pattern); cluster the
  new tests under a `TestDiffHandFillAffordance` or similar class.
- Whether the helper signature returns paired tuples or a paired-fingerprint
  index — planner picks whatever reads cleanly.
- Whether to introduce a HANDFILL-01-specific error code (E405?) when real
  drift is detected on a position the user hand-filled — currently SC#4 says
  the existing `SystemDriftError` still raises, so default is "no new error
  code; reuse SystemDriftError E404". Planner has discretion to propose a
  more specific code if the call-site benefits.

### Folded Todos

- **phase-5.2-diff-empty-collector-handfill.md** — original todo (renamed
  during phase insertion at commit `08e00ba` from
  `diff-empty-collector-as-handfill-affordance.md`). Source: phase-05 UAT
  Test 3 (LIFE-04 hand-fill survival). The todo's proposal directly maps
  to D-60..D-66 above; the implementation sketch and the new-test list in
  the todo's "How to apply (sketch)" §4 are folded into the plan scope.

</decisions>

### Production code (single-file change surface)
- `mlpstorage_py/system_description/diff.py` — the entire change surface:
  - lines 176-184: `_compute_fingerprint` (unchanged — IDENTITY contract preserved)
  - lines 219-289: `diff_node_dict_lists` (insertion point for soft-pair pre-pass)
  - lines 235-238: existing `on_disk_by_fp` / `in_memory_by_fp` indexing
    (preserve as exact-match pass-1 of D-62)
  - lines 242-262: existing orphan-emission loop (becomes pass-3 after
    soft-pair runs)
  - lines 272-280: Pitfall 3(a) SER-02 leaf rule (UNCHANGED — soft-paired
    orphans fall through here so the rule actually fires on hand-fill cases)
- `mlpstorage_py/system_description/auto_generator.py:184-196` —
  `_FINGERPRINT_KEYS` composition (the 11-tuple: 7 scalar dotted-keys + 4
  callable signatures). REFERENCED ONLY; not modified.
- `mlpstorage_py/system_description/auto_generator.py:214-235` —
  `_get_dotted` returns `""` on miss (D-5 convention). This is the empty
  sentinel the soft-pair pass keys on.

<code_context>
## Existing Code Insights

### Reusable Assets
- **Pitfall 3(a) SER-02 leaf rule** at `diff.py:272-280` — already
  implements the "in-memory empty + on-disk non-empty → keep submitter's
  value" semantics correctly. Phase 5.2's soft-pair pre-pass exists
  precisely to let this rule fire on fingerprint-field cases that today
  orphan before reaching leaf comparison. **No change needed to this
  block** — it's the punchline of the soft-pair design.
- **`_compute_fingerprint(stanza)`** at `diff.py:176-184` — returns the
  11-tuple per D-38. The soft-pair pass calls this to identify which
  positions are `""` on the in-memory side. **Contract preserved** —
  general-purpose callers (dedup, sort, render via
  `_render_fingerprint`) see no change.
- **`_render_fingerprint(fp)`** at `diff.py:192-211` — handles multi-value
  sysctl leaves with literal tabs per D-41. Soft-pair logic does not need
  rendering; it compares tuples directly. The reverse-direction INFO log
  (D-60) MAY use this helper for the "was '' on disk" phrasing if the
  empty position is a signature; for scalar positions, the value is
  trivially a string.
- **`_flatten_to_paths(value, prefix)`** at `diff.py:142-167` — flattens
  nested dict/list structures into `(path, leaf)` tuples. Already used by
  the existing leaf-comparison pass; soft-paired orphans flow through
  this same flattening.
- **`_FINGERPRINT_KEYS`** at `auto_generator.py:184-196` — the 11-tuple
  composition. Soft-pair pass must NOT mutate this; it only inspects
  which positions are scalar (the first 7) vs callable signatures (the
  last 4) to apply D-61 strict-match for signatures.

### Established Patterns
- **D-22 mixed-type defense (`key=repr`)** at `diff.py:238` — the union
  of fingerprints is sorted by `repr()` to defend against tuple
  comparison failures when sysctl tuples contain mixed types. Soft-pair
  result iteration must respect this same defensive sort to keep DiffEntry
  paths deterministic across runs.
- **`_SENTINEL_ABSENT` module-level object** at `diff.py:96` —
  distinguishes "key absent" from "key present but empty". The soft-pair
  pass works on the fingerprint POSITION level (which is always present
  in the 11-tuple — there is no "absent" fingerprint position; only "")
  so this sentinel is not needed inside soft-pair. It IS used by the
  downstream leaf comparison the soft-paired clients fall through to.
- **D-12 writer gate (`args.command == 'run'`)** at
  `auto_generator.py:806-808` — irrelevant to soft-pair (diff layer is
  read-only). Mentioned only because the user might worry "does soft-pair
  write anything?" — answer: no.
- **TDD RED-first commit cadence** — established by Phase 5.1's
  `(05-fix-cap01-datagen)`, `(05.1-fix-cap02-stdout-transport)`,
  `(05.1-fix-cap03-orgname)`, `(05.1-fix-cap04-tag-output)` scopes.
  Phase 5.2 uses scope `(05.2-handfill)` per D-65.

### Integration Points
- **Single call site**: `diff_node_dict_lists` at `diff.py:219` is called
  from `mlpstorage_py/system_description/lifecycle.py` (or wherever the
  Phase 5 diff lifecycle is wired — confirm during planning). The soft-pair
  pass is invisible to the caller — `DiffResult.empty` continues to be the
  signal for "no drift; proceed silently".
- **D-60 INFO log** integrates with the existing logger pipeline
  (`logging.getLogger("mlpstorage")` style; check 05-CONTEXT.md /
  `cluster_collector.py` for the actual logger name and INFO-level
  conventions used elsewhere in the project — the planner should match
  existing operator-facing log style).
- **No new error code introduced** — D-66 Claude's Discretion noted that
  the planner MAY propose E405 for hand-fill-position drift, but default
  is "reuse existing E404 SystemDriftError" per SC#4.

</code_context>

<specifics>
## Specific Ideas

- **Canonical example used throughout discussion**: `chassis.model_name`
  on a generic Linux box without parseable DMI strings — collector returns
  `""`; user hand-fills `"Latitude 7420"`; on next re-run, collector
  still returns `""` because the dmidecode output didn't change. Today:
  fingerprint differs → orphan → SystemDriftError E404. After 5.2:
  fingerprint differs only at the empty position → soft-pair → leaf
  Pitfall 3(a) fires → DiffResult.empty → no error.
- **Multi-client topology safety**: the test corpus should include at
  least one case with 3+ distinct on-disk clients where one in-memory
  orphan has multiple soft-pair candidates due to overlapping empty
  positions. D-63 says this falls back to orphan emission (conservative)
  — the test locks that behavior.
- **Reverse-direction example**: the dev box reboots with a new
  dmidecode parser that suddenly resolves `chassis.model_name` to
  `"Dell Latitude E5450"`. On-disk was `""` (collector empty + user
  didn't hand-fill). After 5.2: INFO log on operator's terminal, no
  drift, on-disk file unchanged. User reads the log and chooses whether
  to hand-edit the YAML.

</specifics>

<deferred>
## Deferred Ideas

- **Env / sysctl / D-38 fingerprint audit** — separate concern, tracked in
  `.planning/todos/pending/phase-future-env-sysctl-fingerprint-audit.md`
  (originally pre-filed as Phase 5.2, demoted to post-v1.0 / Phase 5.3
  candidate during 5.2 insertion at commit `08e00ba`). The immediate OMPI
  denylist is already shipped (commits `2bb8a38` RED + `0822c81` GREEN);
  the broader cross-launcher audit (PMI_*, SLURM_*, PALS_*, HYDRA_*,
  PBS_*, LSB_*, KUBE_* + sysctl auto-tune curation) is non-blocking for
  the 2026-06-26 freeze.

- **`TestOpenClosedCLIFlags` test-rot cleanup** —
  `.planning/todos/pending/migrate-or-delete-test-open-closed-cli-flags.md`
  (severity minor, source: PR-#412 test-rot follow-up). Unrelated to
  diff-layer work; standalone cleanup. Not folded.

- **HANDFILL-02 candidate: hand-fill `friendly_description` survives drift
  reports too** — currently `friendly_description` is OUT of the 11-tuple
  fingerprint (so LIFE-04 Pitfall 3(a) already handles it). If a future
  decision puts it INTO the fingerprint for stronger identity guarantees,
  the soft-pair logic from this phase would automatically cover it (no
  re-work needed). Logged as a forward-compat note, not a separate todo.

- **CLI flag to force a soft-pair re-run report** (e.g.,
  `mlpstorage validate --show-handfill-coverage`) — operator-facing
  visibility into which positions are hand-filled vs collector-derived.
  Out of scope for 5.2; logged as a v1.1 idea.

### Reviewed Todos (not folded)
- `phase-future-env-sysctl-fingerprint-audit.md` — see Deferred Ideas §1
  above. Reviewed during the `cross_reference_todos` step; demoted to
  post-v1.0 during phase insertion.
- `migrate-or-delete-test-open-closed-cli-flags.md` — see Deferred Ideas
  §2 above. Reviewed and confirmed out of scope (test rot, not
  diff-layer).

</deferred>
